mnemonics.title: Remember Register Bank Selection
mnemonics.content: RS1 RS0 = Bank Number (00=0, 01=1, 10=2, 11=3) - Think 'RS1 is MSB, RS0 is LSB' of bank number in binary

mnemonics.title: PSW Flags Reminder
mnemonics.content: CY-AC-F0-RS1-RS0-OV---P (MSB to LSB) - Read as 'Carry Auxiliary Flag Owner RS1 RS0 Overflow Parity'

mnemonics.title: SFR Address Pattern
mnemonics.content: SFRs occupy 80H-FFH: Ports P0-P3 (80,90,A0,B0), Timer (88,89,8A,8B), Serial (98,99), Interrupt (A8,A0,B8)

acronyms.expansion: Accumulator - Primary register for arithmetic/logical operations
acronyms.usage: Used in almost all ALU operations like ADD, SUB, MOV

acronyms.expansion: Program Status Word - Contains flag bits and register bank selection
acronyms.usage: Stores carry, auxiliary carry, overflow, parity flags and RS0/RS1 bits

acronyms.expansion: Stack Pointer - Points to top of stack in internal RAM
acronyms.usage: Pushed during CALL and PUSH, popped during RET and POP

acronyms.expansion: Program Counter - Holds address of next instruction
acronyms.usage: Auto-incremented after each fetch, modified by jump/branch instructions

acronyms.expansion: Data Pointer - 16-bit pointer for external memory access
acronyms.usage: Used with MOVX to access external RAM, and MOVC for look-up tables

acronyms.expansion: Serial Buffer - Holds serial transmit/receive data
acronyms.usage: Writing to SBUF transmits data, reading SBUF receives data

acronyms.expansion: Special Function Register - Registers with specific functions in 8051
acronyms.usage: Controls I/O ports, timers, serial communication, and interrupts
EA bit enables all, individual bits enable specific interrupts

visualTips.visualization: Draw 4 horizontal boxes (banks), each with R0-R7. Bank 0 at 00H-07H, Bank 1 at 08H-0FH, etc. Show RS0/RS1 selection arrows pointing to active bank.
Draw an 8-bit row: CY(7), AC(6), F0(5), RS1(4), RS0(3), OV(2), -(1), P(0). Color-code flag bits vs selection bits.
Create a 128-byte grid (80H-FFH). Fill in: P0=80H, SP=81H, DPL=82H, DPH=83H, PCON=87H, TCON=88H, TMOD=89H, TL0=8AH, etc.

analogies.analogy: Like the main workspace on a desk - you bring papers (data) here to work on them, then store results elsewhere.
Like a bookmark in a book - it always marks the next page (instruction) to read.
Like a stack of plates - SP points to the top plate, you add (push) and remove (pop) from the top.
Like 4 different notepads - you can switch between them instantly to use different sets of R0-R7 registers.
Like an index finger pointing to a specific location in a large library (external memory) - tells where to look next.
